was the 21st season of the [NBA G League](/wiki/NBA_G_League "NBA G League"), the official [minor league basketball](/wiki/Minor_league_basketball "Minor league basketball") organization owned by the [National Basketball Association](/wiki/National_Basketball_Association "National Basketball Association") (NBA). The prior season, the league played a shortened season with 18 teams and all games were held at [ESPN Wide World of Sports Complex](/wiki/ESPN_Wide_World_of_Sports_Complex "ESPN Wide World of Sports Complex") in [Bay Lake, Florida](/wiki/Bay_Lake,_Florida "Bay Lake, Florida"), in similar to the [2020 NBA Bubble](/wiki/2020_NBA_Bubble "2020 NBA Bubble") due to the ongoing restrictions during the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic").[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) The 2021–22 season consisted of two phases: a Showcase Cup from November 5 to December 22, with all 30 teams participating before 28 of the league teams underwent a traditional regular season intended to span 36 games for each team from January 5 to April 2 before postseason. Untimely postponements, however, would lead to a varying level of games played before the end of the season, which meant roughly 31-35 games were played by each team.[[3]](#cite_note-Format-3)

The **2021–22 NBA G League season** was the 21st season of the [NBA G League](/wiki/NBA_G_League "NBA G League"), the official [minor league basketball](/wiki/Minor_league_basketball "Minor league basketball") organization owned by the [National Basketball Association](/wiki/National_Basketball_Association "National Basketball Association") (NBA). The prior season, the league played a shortened season with 18 teams and all games were held at [ESPN Wide World of Sports Complex](/wiki/ESPN_Wide_World_of_Sports_Complex "ESPN Wide World of Sports Complex") in [Bay Lake, Florida](/wiki/Bay_Lake,_Florida "Bay Lake, Florida"), in similar to the [2020 NBA Bubble](/wiki/2020_NBA_Bubble "2020 NBA Bubble") due to the ongoing restrictions during the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic").[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) The 2021–22 season consisted of two phases: a Showcase Cup from November 5 to December 22, with all 30 teams participating before 28 of the league teams underwent a traditional regular season intended to span 36 games for each team from January 5 to April 2 before postseason. Untimely postponements, however, would lead to a varying level of games played before the end of the season, which meant roughly 31-35 games were played by each team.[[3]](#cite_note-Format-3)

## League changes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2021%E2%80%9322_NBA_G_League_season&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: League changes")]

The NBA G League added one new team, had three teams relocate, and two teams rebrand for the 2021–22 season:

* The [Capitanes de la Ciudad de México](/wiki/Capitanes_de_la_Ciudad_de_M%C3%A9xico "Capitanes de la Ciudad de México") were initially planned to start playing in the G League for the 2020–21 season, but pushed back their debut in the G League to the 2021–22 season due to the pandemic.[[4]](#cite_note-4) As the travel restrictions continued in the scheduling of the 2021–22 season, the Capitanes played during the Showcase Cup from November 5 to December 22 and were not able to play any home games in [Mexico City](/wiki/Mexico_City "Mexico City").
* The Canton Charge relocated to [Cleveland](/wiki/Cleveland "Cleveland") to play at [Cleveland State University](/wiki/Cleveland_State_University "Cleveland State University")'s [Wolstein Center](/wiki/Wolstein_Center "Wolstein Center") and were re-branded as the [Cleveland Charge](/wiki/Cleveland_Charge "Cleveland Charge").[[5]](#cite_note-5)
* The [Detroit Pistons](/wiki/Detroit_Pistons "Detroit Pistons") purchased and relocated the [Northern Arizona Suns](/wiki/Northern_Arizona_Suns "Northern Arizona Suns") franchise to become the [Motor City Cruise](/wiki/Motor_City_Cruise "Motor City Cruise") with home games in [Detroit](/wiki/Detroit "Detroit") at [Wayne State Fieldhouse](/wiki/Wayne_State_Fieldhouse "Wayne State Fieldhouse").
* The [New Orleans Pelicans](/wiki/New_Orleans_Pelicans "New Orleans Pelicans") relocated their franchise, previously playing as the Erie BayHawks, to [Birmingham, Alabama](/wiki/Birmingham,_Alabama "Birmingham, Alabama"), as the [Birmingham Squadron](/wiki/Birmingham_Squadron "Birmingham Squadron") following the renovation of [Legacy Arena](/wiki/Legacy_Arena "Legacy Arena").[[6]](#cite_note-6)
* The [Boston Celtics](/wiki/Boston_Celtics "Boston Celtics") rebranded their affiliate, the Maine Red Claws, as the [Maine Celtics](/wiki/Maine_Celtics "Maine Celtics").[[7]](#cite_note-7)
* The [Denver Nuggets](/wiki/Denver_Nuggets "Denver Nuggets") affiliated with the Grand Rapids Drive, which had previously been affiliated with the Pistons, and the Drive rebranded as the [Grand Rapids Gold](/wiki/Grand_Rapids_Gold "Grand Rapids Gold").[[8]](#cite_note-grgold-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)
* Divisions were abolished in place of having the top six teams of each conference make the postseason (the last season with divisions was the [2019–20 NBA G League season](/wiki/2019%E2%80%9320_NBA_G_League_season "2019–20 NBA G League season"), as the 2020–21 season had the top eight of the eighteen teams make the playoffs).

Additionally, the [Westchester Knicks](/wiki/Westchester_Knicks "Westchester Knicks") temporarily relocated their home games from [White Plains, New York](/wiki/White_Plains,_New_York "White Plains, New York"), to [Bridgeport, Connecticut](/wiki/Bridgeport,_Connecticut "Bridgeport, Connecticut"), as their home arena, the [Westchester County Center](/wiki/Westchester_County_Center "Westchester County Center"), was still being used as a COVID-19 vaccination site.[[10]](#cite_note-10) The Capitanes de la Ciudad de México also set up their season in [Fort Worth, Texas](/wiki/Fort_Worth,_Texas "Fort Worth, Texas") as a temporary home for this season in order to play in the G League this season without circumventing border restrictions at the time.

The league changed its season format to be played in two stages: a 14-game Showcase Cup and then an intended 36-game regular season with a playoff. All 30 teams played in the Showcase Cup that ended with the NBA G League Winter Showcase on the weekend of December 19 to 22. The regular season was then set to start on December 27, but was then pushed back to January 5.[[11]](#cite_note-11) The league standings reset for the start of the regular season for qualifying for the playoffs. The [NBA G League Ignite](/wiki/NBA_G_League_Ignite "NBA G League Ignite") and [Capitanes de la Ciudad de México](/wiki/Capitanes_de_la_Ciudad_de_M%C3%A9xico "Capitanes de la Ciudad de México") will not participate in the regular season or playoffs, but will play a few exhibition games that do not count towards the other teams standings.[[3]](#cite_note-Format-3)

### Other changes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2021%E2%80%9322_NBA_G_League_season&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Other changes")]

This season is the first for [Wilson](/wiki/Wilson_Sporting_Goods "Wilson Sporting Goods") as the G League's official ball brand. On May 14, 2020, the NBA announced a multi-year partnership with Wilson as the manufacturer and supplier of G League game balls,[[12]](#cite_note-12) to succeed [Spalding](/wiki/Spalding_(company) "Spalding (company)"), which supplied the league since 2001 as the National Basketball Development League (NBDL).[[13]](#cite_note-13)

## Playoffs

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2021%E2%80%9322_NBA_G_League_season&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Playoffs")]

* Quarterfinals: April 5 (one game)[[15]](#cite_note-15)
* Semifinals: April 7/8 (one game)
* Conference Finals: April 9 & 10 (one game)
* Finals: April 12 & April 14 (best-of-three)

For the fifth time in the best-of-three series era and for the first time since [2018](/wiki/2017%E2%80%9318_NBA_G_League_season#Playoffs "2017–18 NBA G League season"), a team went undefeated in postseason play, as the Rio Grande Valley Vipers went 4–0 to win their fourth league title, defeating the [Delaware Blue Coats](/wiki/Delaware_Blue_Coats "Delaware Blue Coats") in two games. The Vipers set a couple of records during the G League Finals, most notably in Game 1 when they scored 145 points in a 145–128 victory, which was the most points scored by a team in G League Finals history. [Trevelin Queen](/wiki/Trevelin_Queen "Trevelin Queen") scored 44 points, the second most scored in a Final; two [triple-doubles](/wiki/Triple-double "Triple-double") were recorded by the Vipers: [Daishen Nix](/wiki/Daishen_Nix "Daishen Nix") scored 31 points, 12 rebounds, and 11 assists and [Anthony Lamb](/wiki/Anthony_Lamb_(basketball) "Anthony Lamb (basketball)") scored 10 points, 10 rebounds, and 10 assists. This was the first time a triple-double had been recorded in the League since 2013 and it was the first Finals game to feature two triple-doubles in the same game. [Mfiondu Kabengele](/wiki/Mfiondu_Kabengele "Mfiondu Kabengele") recorded eight blocks, a Finals record. The Blue Coats became the first team since the [Santa Cruz Warriors](/wiki/Santa_Cruz_Warriors "Santa Cruz Warriors") (2013, 2014) to lose back-to-back G League Finals.[[16]](#cite_note-16)[[17]](#cite_note-17)[[18]](#cite_note-18)[[19]](#cite_note-19)[[20]](#cite_note-20)

## Notable occurrences

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2021%E2%80%9322_NBA_G_League_season&action=edit&section=23 "Edit section: Notable occurrences")]

* On December 26, 2021, [Deividas Sirvydis](/wiki/Deividas_Sirvydis "Deividas Sirvydis") of the [Motor City Cruise](/wiki/Motor_City_Cruise "Motor City Cruise") received a call-up by the [Detroit Pistons](/wiki/Detroit_Pistons "Detroit Pistons"), making him the 64th player to be called up that season and breaking the record of 63 call-ups in the [2014–15 season](/wiki/2014%E2%80%9315_NBA_Development_League_season "2014–15 NBA Development League season").[[25]](#cite_note-25)
